source.title: Bidirectional switch of the valence associated with a hippocampal contextual memory engram.

confidence_rationale: Nature optogenetics study showing hippocampal (DG) contextual engram can switch valence, while BLA engram cannot switch. BLA-DG functional connectivity changes accompany the valence switch. This is mixed evidence for MECH-074d: it supports that BLA provides stable valence attribution (consistent with harm-PE-gating the remap), but uses explicit optogenetic whole-engram manipulation rather than the PE-triggered partial remap that MECH-074d proposes.
failure_signatures: BLA engram valence cannot be switched in this paradigm, suggesting BLA valence tagging may be more rigid than MECH-074d's partial-remap-with-attribution model allows.
Whole-engram manipulation not equivalent to the ~30-35% partial remap triggered by harm-PE spike in MECH-074d; valence switch is binary not graded.

mapping.source_claim_statement: DG contextual engram cells can switch valence (fear<->reward) by reactivating under opposite conditioning. BLA engram cells cannot switch valence. Valence switch in DG is accompanied by changed DG-BLA functional connectivity.
mapping.ree_translation: BLA-labelled codes have stable valence attribution, consistent with MECH-074d's claim that BLA-attribution gates the remap (only codes flagged by predictor-attribution are remapped). DG-mediated partial remap in MECH-074d corresponds to the DG plasticity shown here. BLA-DG connectivity change accompanies remap, consistent with the attribution-gating architecture.
mapping.mapping_caveat: This paper uses whole-engram optogenetic reactivation during opposite valence conditioning -- an explicit experimental paradigm -- rather than the spontaneous harm-PE-triggered partial remap of MECH-074d. The rigidity of BLA engram valence could constrain MECH-074d's remap amplitude more than the 30-35% overwrite estimate.
mapping.source_context: C57BL/6 mice, ChR2 engram tagging in DG or BLA, optogenetic place avoidance/preference tests.

confidence_components.notes: Nature, MIT Tonegawa lab, landmark engram paper. Mapping requires bridging from explicit optogenetic paradigm to naturalistic PE-triggered partial remap.
